rulings.Case Title: NATALIE BUFORD et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Court Approval Of Settlement As Being In Good Faith, And For Dismissal
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR TUESDAY, AUGUST 30, 2011 IN DEPARTMENT 220 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 1. Intervenors¿ motion for court approval of settlement as being in good faith and for dismissal is granted in its entirety. The settlement between Rich-Tex and Mr. Buford was in ¿good faith¿ as that phrase is used in the context of a good faith settlement per CCP 877.6. That settlement bars third parties¿ implied and equitable indemnity claims against Rich-Tex. The good faith settlement also bars Marc Paul¿s claim under CCP 1021.6 because the 1021.6 claim is contingent on Marc Pau¿s recovery on its implied indemnity claim, which is now barred. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR TUESDAY, AUGUST 30, 2011 IN DEPARTMENT 220 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 8. Defendant Tingue, Brown & Co.¿s general demurrer to plaintiffs¿ first amended complaint is sustained with ten days leave to amend to give plaintiffs an opportunity, if they are able to do so, to allege facts sufficient to meet the requirements of Bockrath. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: SHANE BRANDON et al VS. CERTAINTEED CORPORATION
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION And Notice Of Motion And Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR TUESDAY, AUGUST 30, 2011 IN DEPARTMENT 220 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 11. Defendant U.S. Borax, Inc.¿s motion for judgment on the pleadings is denied. Looking solely to the four corners of the complaint, including exhibit A thereto, and anything that may be judicially noticed, defendant has not shown that plaintiffs were aware of the facts creating defendant¿s liability any earlier than when they amended their complaint in March 2011 to substitute defendant in place of a Doe. = (220/HEK)
